---
title: "Mauscript_code"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-20"
---

This document contains the code required to replicate the analyses conducted in 'BMA distribution as an associate and predictor of type 2 diabetes: a UK Biobank study'. Please note that the following code assumes access to certain files that are required for the analyses to run (see Data availability section in the main text) and that the file and column names are the same as those specified in each chunk of code.

1. BMFF data cleaning, quality-control and PCA (including PCA figures)
```{r}
#Batch 1

#UKB withdrawn participants
colnames(ukb_withdraw) = "eid"

#Spine - remove NAs, withrawals and SD (voxels) < 3*SD below mean
Spine_BMFF_df = na.omit(spine_BMFF_31748)
mean(Spine_BMFF_df$No.voxels)
sd(Spine_BMFF_df$No.voxels)
Spine_BMFF_df = subset(Spine_BMFF_df, No.voxels >612.057)
Spine_BMFF_df <- Spine_BMFF_df %>%
  filter(!eid %in% ukb_withdraw$eid)

Batch_1_spine_BMFF_df = Spine_BMFF_df

export(Batch_1_spine_BMFF_df, "Batch_1_spine_BMFF_df.csv")

#Femoral head
Femoral_head_BMFF_df = na.omit(head_BMFF_29939)
Femoral_head_BMFF_df = subset(Femoral_head_BMFF_df, No.voxels >=20)
Femoral_head_BMFF_df <- Femoral_head_BMFF_df %>%
  filter(!eid %in% ukb_withdraw$eid)

export(Femoral_head_BMFF_df, "Batch_1_femoral_head_BMFF_df.csv")

#Total hip
Total_hip_BMFF_df = na.omit(totalhip_BMFF_29186)
Total_hip_BMFF_df = subset(Total_hip_BMFF_df, No.voxels >=20)
Total_hip_BMFF_df <- Total_hip_BMFF_df %>%
  filter(!eid %in% ukb_withdraw$eid)

export(Total_hip_BMFF_df, "Batch_1_total_hip_BMFF_df.csv")

#Diaphysis
Diaphysis_BMFF_df = na.omit(diaphysis_BMFF_29358)
Diaphysis_BMFF_df = subset(Diaphysis_BMFF_df, No.voxels >=20)
Diaphysis_BMFF_df <- Diaphysis_BMFF_df %>%
  filter(!eid %in% ukb_withdraw$eid)

export(Diaphysis_BMFF_df, "Batch_1_diaphysis_BMFF_df.csv")

# List of data frames
dfs <- list(Spine_BMFF_df[,c(1,4,8)], Femoral_head_BMFF_df[,c(1,4,8)], Total_hip_BMFF_df[,c(1,4,8)], Diaphysis_BMFF_df[,c(1,4,8)])

# Merging all data frames by 'eid'
BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df <- reduce(dfs, function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "eid"))
colnames(BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df) = c("eid", "Spine_BMFF", "Spine_voxels", "Femoral_head_BMFF", "Femoral_head_voxels", "Total_hip_BMFF", "Total_hip_voxels", "Diaphysis_BMFF", "Diaphysis_voxels")

Batch_1_BMFF_outlier_PCA = prcomp(BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df[,c(2,4,6,8)], scale = TRUE)
Batch_1_BMFF_outlier_PCA_df = data.frame(Batch_1_BMFF_outlier_PCA$x)
Batch_1_BMFF_outlier_PCA_df = Batch_1_BMFF_outlier_PCA_df*-1
BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df = cbind(BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df, Batch_1_BMFF_outlier_PCA_df)

BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df %>% ggplot(aes(x=Spine_BMFF, y=PC2))+
  geom_point()+
  theme_bw()

BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df %>% ggplot(aes(x=Femoral_head_BMFF, y=PC2))+
  geom_point()+
  theme_bw()

BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df %>% ggplot(aes(x=PC1, y=PC2))+
  geom_point()+
  theme_bw()

export(BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df, "Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df.csv")
Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df$rt_Spine_BMFF = RankNorm(Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df$Spine_BMFF)
Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df$rt_Femoral_head_BMFF = RankNorm(Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df$Femoral_head_BMFF)
Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df$rt_Total_hip_BMFF = RankNorm(Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df$Total_hip_BMFF)
Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df$rt_Diaphysis_BMFF = RankNorm(Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df$Diaphysis_BMFF)
Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df$rt_PC2 = RankNorm(Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df$PC2)

#Reshape data to long format
long_df <- Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(rt_Spine_BMFF, rt_Diaphysis_BMFF, rt_Femoral_head_BMFF, rt_Total_hip_BMFF),
               names_to = "Measurement",
               values_to = "Value")

BMFF_PC_lm = Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df %>% lm(rt_PC2~rt_Spine_BMFF, data=.)
summary(BMFF_PC_lm)

long_df$Measurement <- factor(long_df$Measurement, levels=c('rt_Spine_BMFF', 'rt_Femoral_head_BMFF', 'rt_Total_hip_BMFF', 'rt_Diaphysis_BMFF'))

#Plot all in one
ggplot(long_df, aes(x = Value, y = rt_PC2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color = Measurement), method = lm) +
  labs(x = "BMFF (normalised)", y = "PC2 (normalised)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 5, b = 5)),
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")) +
  theme(legend.title=element_blank())+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("rt_Spine_BMFF" = "red",
                                "rt_Femoral_head_BMFF" = "orange",
                                "rt_Total_hip_BMFF" = "steelblue",
                                "rt_Diaphysis_BMFF" = "#7a384c"), 
                     labels = c("rt_Spine_BMFF" = "Spine BMFF :\nβ = 0.88, R² = 0.78, p<2e-16",
                                "rt_Femoral_head_BMFF" = "Femoral head BMFF :\nβ = -0.22, R² = 0.05, p<2e-16",
                                "rt_Total_hip_BMFF" = "Total hip BMFF :\nβ = -0.15, R² = 0.02, p<2e-16",
                                "rt_Diaphysis_BMFF" = "Diaphysis BMFF :\nβ = -0.16, R² = 0.03, p<2e-16"))


#PCA diagnostic plots
fviz_eig(Batch_1_BMFF_outlier_PCA, addlabels = TRUE)

#Show percentage contribution to PC1 and PC2 on bar plot
BMFF_1_PCA_loading_df = Batch_1_BMFF_outlier_PCA$rotation
BMFF_1_PCA_loading_df = BMFF_1_PCA_loading_df*100
BMFF_1_PCA_loading_df <- as.data.frame(BMFF_1_PCA_loading_df)
BMFF_1_PCA_loading_df$Variable <- rownames(BMFF_1_PCA_loading_df)
BMFF_1_PCA_loading_df$Variable = c("Spine BMFF", "Femoral head BMFF", "Total hip BMFF", "Diaphysis BMFF")

library(reshape2)
BMFF_1_PCA_loading_df <- melt(BMFF_1_PCA_loading_df, id.vars = "Variable", variable.name = "PrincipalComponent", value.name = "Percentage")

subset(BMFF_1_PCA_loading_df, PrincipalComponent == "PC2" | PrincipalComponent == "PC1") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Variable, y = Percentage, fill = PrincipalComponent)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", Percentage)), 
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
            fontface = "bold") +
  labs(y = "% Contribution", x = "") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.title = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df %>% ggplot(aes(x=PC1, y=PC2))+
  geom_point()+
  labs(x="PC1 (60.4%)", y="PC2 (21.4%)")+
  theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face="bold"))+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(face="bold"))+
  theme(legend.text = element_text(face = "bold"))+
  theme(legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"))+
  theme(axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"))


#Batch 2
#UKB withdrawn participants
colnames(ukb_withdraw) = "eid"

#Spine - remove NAs, withrawals and SD (voxels) < 3*SD below mean
Spine_BMFF_df = na.omit(spine_BMFF_18920)
mean(Spine_BMFF_df$No.voxels)
sd(Spine_BMFF_df$No.voxels)
Spine_BMFF_df = subset(Spine_BMFF_df, No.voxels >634.3218)
Spine_BMFF_df <- Spine_BMFF_df %>%
  filter(!eid %in% ukb_withdraw$eid)

Batch_2_spine_BMFF_df = Spine_BMFF_df

export(Batch_2_spine_BMFF_df, "Batch_2_spine_BMFF_df.csv")

#Femoral head
Femoral_head_BMFF_df = na.omit(head_BMFF_17522)
Femoral_head_BMFF_df = subset(Femoral_head_BMFF_df, No.voxels >=20)
Femoral_head_BMFF_df <- Femoral_head_BMFF_df %>%
  filter(!eid %in% ukb_withdraw$eid)

export(Femoral_head_BMFF_df, "Batch_2_femoral_head_BMFF_df.csv")

#Total hip
Total_hip_BMFF_df = na.omit(TP_BMFF_18065)
Total_hip_BMFF_df = subset(Total_hip_BMFF_df, No.voxels >=20)
Total_hip_BMFF_df <- Total_hip_BMFF_df %>%
  filter(!eid %in% ukb_withdraw$eid)

export(Total_hip_BMFF_df, "Batch_2_total_hip_BMFF_df.csv")

#Diaphysis
Diaphysis_BMFF_df = na.omit(diaphysis_BMFF_17160)
Diaphysis_BMFF_df = subset(Diaphysis_BMFF_df, No.voxels >=20)
Diaphysis_BMFF_df <- Diaphysis_BMFF_df %>%
  filter(!eid %in% ukb_withdraw$eid)

export(Diaphysis_BMFF_df, "Batch_2_diaphysis_BMFF_df.csv")

# List of data frames
dfs <- list(Spine_BMFF_df[,c(1,4,8)], Femoral_head_BMFF_df[,c(1,4,8)], Total_hip_BMFF_df[,c(1,4,8)], Diaphysis_BMFF_df[,c(1,4,8)])

# Merging all data frames by 'eid'
BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df <- reduce(dfs, function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "eid"))
colnames(BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df) = c("eid", "Spine_BMFF", "Spine_voxels", "Femoral_head_BMFF", "Femoral_head_voxels", "Total_hip_BMFF", "Total_hip_voxels", "Diaphysis_BMFF", "Diaphysis_voxels")

Batch_2_BMFF_outlier_PCA = prcomp(BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df[,c(2,4,6,8)], scale = TRUE)
Batch_2_BMFF_outlier_PCA_df = data.frame(Batch_2_BMFF_outlier_PCA$x)
Batch_2_BMFF_outlier_PCA_df$PC1 = Batch_2_BMFF_outlier_PCA_df$PC1*-1
BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df = cbind(BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df, Batch_2_BMFF_outlier_PCA_df)

BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df %>% ggplot(aes(x=Spine_BMFF, y=PC2))+
  geom_point()+
  theme_bw()

BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df %>% ggplot(aes(x=PC1, y=PC2))+
  geom_point()+
  theme_bw()

export(BMFF_outlier_inpsection_df, "Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df.csv")
Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df$rt_Spine_BMFF = RankNorm(Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df$Spine_BMFF)
Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df$rt_Femoral_head_BMFF = RankNorm(Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df$Femoral_head_BMFF)
Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df$rt_Total_hip_BMFF = RankNorm(Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df$Total_hip_BMFF)
Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df$rt_Diaphysis_BMFF = RankNorm(Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df$Diaphysis_BMFF)
Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df$rt_PC2 = RankNorm(Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df$PC2)

#Reshape data to long format
long_df <- Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(rt_Spine_BMFF, rt_Diaphysis_BMFF, rt_Femoral_head_BMFF, rt_Total_hip_BMFF),
               names_to = "Measurement",
               values_to = "Value")

long_df$Measurement <- factor(long_df$Measurement, levels=c('rt_Spine_BMFF', 'rt_Femoral_head_BMFF', 'rt_Total_hip_BMFF', 'rt_Diaphysis_BMFF'))

BMFF_PC_lm = Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df %>% lm(rt_PC2~rt_Diaphysis_BMFF, data=.)
summary(BMFF_PC_lm)

#Plot all in one
ggplot(long_df, aes(x = Value, y = rt_PC2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color = Measurement), method = lm) +
  labs(x = "BMFF (normalised)", y = "PC2 (normalised)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 5, b = 5)),
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")) +
  theme(legend.title=element_blank())+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("rt_Spine_BMFF" = "red",
                                "rt_Femoral_head_BMFF" = "orange",
                                "rt_Total_hip_BMFF" = "steelblue",
                                "rt_Diaphysis_BMFF" = "#7a384c"), 
                     labels = c("rt_Spine_BMFF" = "Spine BMFF :\nβ = 0.89, R² = 0.79, p<2e-16",
                                "rt_Femoral_head_BMFF" = "Femoral head BMFF :\nβ = -0.22, R² = 0.05, p<2e-16",
                                "rt_Total_hip_BMFF" = "Total hip BMFF :\nβ = -0.15, R² = 0.02, p<2e-16",
                                "rt_Diaphysis_BMFF" = "Diaphysis BMFF :\nβ = -0.14, R² = 0.02, p<2e-16"))


#PCA diagnostic plots
fviz_eig(Batch_2_BMFF_outlier_PCA, addlabels = TRUE)

#Show percentage contribution to PC1 and PC2 on bar plot
BMFF_2_PCA_loading_df = Batch_2_BMFF_outlier_PCA$rotation
BMFF_2_PCA_loading_df = BMFF_2_PCA_loading_df*100
BMFF_2_PCA_loading_df <- as.data.frame(BMFF_2_PCA_loading_df)
BMFF_2_PCA_loading_df$Variable <- rownames(BMFF_2_PCA_loading_df)
BMFF_2_PCA_loading_df$Variable = c("Spine BMFF", "Femoral head BMFF", "Total hip BMFF", "Diaphysis BMFF")

library(reshape2)
BMFF_2_PCA_loading_df <- melt(BMFF_2_PCA_loading_df, id.vars = "Variable", variable.name = "PrincipalComponent", value.name = "Percentage")

subset(BMFF_2_PCA_loading_df, PrincipalComponent == "PC2" | PrincipalComponent == "PC1") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Variable, y = Percentage, fill = PrincipalComponent)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", Percentage)), 
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
            fontface = "bold") +
  labs(y = "% Contribution", x = "") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.title = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df %>% ggplot(aes(x=PC1, y=PC2))+
  geom_point()+
  labs(x="PC1 (58.0%)", y="PC2 (21.8%)")+
  theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face="bold"))+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(face="bold"))+
  theme(legend.text = element_text(face = "bold"))+
  theme(legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"))+
  theme(axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

#Combine the above data frames: Since the PCA for batch 1 and batch 2 produced similar outcomes, the two batches were first combined prior to carrying out the final PCA. Combining the two batches together prior to PCA also ensures that the PCA is being carried out on the largest possible sample size, which is beneficial for several reasons, including the fact that a larger and more diverse data set allows for more variation to be captured by each principal component whilst reducing the imprecisions of the factor loadings (the correlation between each input variable and principal component) and eigenvalues (the amount of variance explained by each principal component) (10.1515/eko-2016-0014) Furthermore, as the central aim of the current project is to investigate the implications of bone marrow adiposity distribution across the entire cohort of participants (to maximize the statistical power for various downstream analyses), the PCA was performed on the complete data set after joining batches 1 and 2 together. 

BMFF_PCA_df = rbind(Batch_1_BMFF_PCA_df, Batch_2_BMFF_PCA_df)
colnames(BMFF_PCA_df)[c(10,11,12,13)] = c('batch_PC1', 'batch_PC2', 'batch_PC3', 'batch_PC4')

BMFF_PCA = prcomp(BMFF_PCA_df[,c(2,4,6,8)], scale = TRUE)
BMFF_PCA_analysis_df = data.frame(BMFF_PCA$x)
BMFF_PCA_df = cbind(BMFF_PCA_df, BMFF_PCA_analysis_df)

fviz_eig(BMFF_PCA, addlabels = TRUE)

#Show percentage contribution to PC1 and PC2 on bar plot
BMFF_PCA_loading_df = BMFF_PCA$rotation
BMFF_PCA_loading_df = BMFF_PCA_loading_df*100
BMFF_PCA_loading_df <- as.data.frame(BMFF_PCA_loading_df)
BMFF_PCA_loading_df$Variable <- rownames(BMFF_PCA_loading_df)
BMFF_PCA_loading_df$Variable = c("Spine BMFF", "Femoral head BMFF", "Total hip BMFF", "Diaphysis BMFF")

library(reshape2)
BMFF_PCA_loading_df <- melt(BMFF_PCA_loading_df, id.vars = "Variable", variable.name = "PrincipalComponent", value.name = "Percentage")

subset(BMFF_PCA_loading_df, PrincipalComponent == "PC2" | PrincipalComponent == "PC1") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Variable, y = Percentage, fill = PrincipalComponent)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", Percentage)), 
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
            fontface = "bold") +
  labs(y = "% Contribution", x = "") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.title = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

BMFF_PCA_df %>% ggplot(aes(x=PC1x, y=PC2x))+
  geom_point()+
  labs(x="PC1 (59.6%)", y="PC2 (21.5%)")+
  theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face="bold"))+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(face="bold"))+
  theme(legend.text = element_text(face = "bold"))+
  theme(legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"))+
  theme(axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

BMFF_PCA_df %>% ggplot(aes(x=Spine_BMFF, y=PC2x))+
  geom_point()+
  theme_bw()

BMFF_PCA_df %>% ggplot(aes(x=Femoral_head_BMFF, y=PC2))+
  geom_point()+
  theme_bw()

BMFF_PCA_df %>% ggplot(aes(x=PC1, y=PC2))+
  geom_point()+
  theme_bw()

#Reshape data to long format
export(BMFF_PCA_df, "BMFF_PCA_df.csv")

long_df <- BMFF_PCA_df[,-c(10:13)] %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(rt_Spine_BMFF, rt_Diaphysis_BMFF, rt_Femoral_head_BMFF, rt_Total_hip_BMFF),
               names_to = "Measurement",
               values_to = "Value")

BMFF_PC_lm = BMFF_PCA_df %>% lm(rt_PC2~rt_Diaphysis_BMFF, data=.)
summary(BMFF_PC_lm)

long_df$Measurement <- factor(long_df$Measurement, levels=c('rt_Spine_BMFF', 'rt_Femoral_head_BMFF', 'rt_Total_hip_BMFF', 'rt_Diaphysis_BMFF'))

#Plot all in one
ggplot(long_df, aes(x = Value, y = rt_PC2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color = Measurement), method = lm) +
  labs(x = "BMFF (normalised)", y = "PC2 (normalised)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 5, b = 5)),
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")) +
  theme(legend.title=element_blank())+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("rt_Spine_BMFF" = "red",
                                "rt_Femoral_head_BMFF" = "orange",
                                "rt_Total_hip_BMFF" = "steelblue",
                                "rt_Diaphysis_BMFF" = "#7a384c"), 
                     labels = c("rt_Spine_BMFF" = "Spine BMFF :\nβ = 0.88, R² = 0.78, p<2e-16",
                                "rt_Femoral_head_BMFF" = "Femoral head BMFF :\nβ = -0.22, R² = 0.05, p<2e-16",
                                "rt_Total_hip_BMFF" = "Total hip BMFF :\nβ = -0.15, R² = 0.02, p<2e-16",
                                "rt_Diaphysis_BMFF" = "Diaphysis BMFF :\nβ = -0.15, R² = 0.02, p<2e-16"))

#No regression lines - for figure 1 manuscript publication

long_df <- BMFF_PCA_df[,c(14:18)] %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(rt_Spine_BMFF, rt_Diaphysis_BMFF, rt_Femoral_head_BMFF, rt_Total_hip_BMFF),
               names_to = "Measurement",
               values_to = "Value")

ggplot(long_df, aes(x = Value, y = rt_PC2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, col=long_df$Measurement) +
  labs(x = "BMFF (normalised)", y = "PC2 (normalised)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 5, b = 5)),
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")) +
  theme(legend.title=element_blank())

meas_cols <- c(
  'rt_Spine_BMFF'        = 'darkred',
  'rt_Femoral_head_BMFF' = 'darkblue',
  'rt_Total_hip_BMFF'    = 'darkblue',
  'rt_Diaphysis_BMFF'    = 'darkblue'
)

ggplot(long_df, aes(x = Value, y = rt_PC2, color = Measurement)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = meas_cols) +
  labs(x = "BMFF (normalised)", y = "PC2 (normalised)", color = NULL) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text  = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.position="none",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold")
  )

#Heatmap/correllelogram
PC2_BMFF_corr_df = BMFF_PCA_df[,c(14:17,23)]
colnames(PC2_BMFF_corr_df) = c("Spine BMFF", "Femoral head BMFF", "Total hip BMFF", "Diaphysis BMFF", "PC2")

#Correlation analysis
PC2_BMFF_corr <- cor(PC2_BMFF_corr_df)

#Including PC2 
ggcorrplot(PC2_BMFF_corr,
           hc.order = TRUE,
           type = "lower",
           lab = TRUE) +
  theme(
    text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text("r" , face = "bold")
  )

ggcorrplot(PC2_BMFF_corr,
           hc.order = TRUE,
           type = "lower",
           lab = TRUE) +
  labs(fill = "r") +
  theme(
    text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

autoplot(BMFF_PCA, data = BMFF_PCA_analysis_df, loadings = TRUE,
         loadings.label = TRUE)

#Highlight the biological outliers to the final score plot
BMFF_PCA_df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = PC1x, y = PC2x)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_point(data = subset(bio_outliers_BMFF_PCA, Femoral_head_BMFF<60), aes(x = PC1x, y = PC2x), col = 'red', size=3) +
  geom_point(data = subset(bio_outliers_BMFF_PCA, Total_hip_BMFF<60), aes(x = PC1x, y = PC2x), col = 'steelblue', size=3) +
  labs(x = "PC1 (59.6%)", y = "PC2 (21.5%)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

BMFF_PCA_df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = PC1x, y = PC2x)) +
  geom_point(color = "darkgray", alpha = 0.75) +
  labs(x = "PC1 (59.6%)", y = "PC2 (21.5%)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold")
  )

#Create final data frames for each skeletal region
Spine_BMFF_final_df = rbind(Batch_1_spine_BMFF_df, Batch_2_spine_BMFF_df)
export(Spine_BMFF_final_df, "Spine_BMFF_final_df.csv")

Femoral_head_BMFF_final_df = rbind(Batch_1_femoral_head_BMFF_df, Batch_2_femoral_head_BMFF_df)
export(Femoral_head_BMFF_final_df, "Femoral_head_BMFF_final_df.csv")

Total_hip_BMFF_final_df = rbind(Batch_1_total_hip_BMFF_df, Batch_2_total_hip_BMFF_df)
export(Total_hip_BMFF_final_df, "Total_hip_BMFF_final_df.csv")

Diaphysis_BMFF_final_df = rbind(Batch_1_diaphysis_BMFF_df, Batch_2_diaphysis_BMFF_df)
export(Diaphysis_BMFF_final_df, "Diaphysis_BMFF_final_df.csv")
```


2. Generate data frames with imputed covariates for adjustment models 1, 2 and 3: anthropometric, MRI and DXA derived covariates are cleaned (removing extreme outliers; outside median ± 3×IQR). See section 2.4 of the ESM for more details.

2.1 Spine BMFF
```{r}
Spine_BMFF_final_df$rt_BMFF = RankNorm(Spine_BMFF_final_df$Mean.fat)

#Merge all of the outcome variables with Spine BMFF into one data frame
data_frames <- list(
  Spine_BMFF_final_df[,c(1,4,9)],
  Age_sex_imaging_df,
  BMI_imaging_df,
  WHR_imaging_df,
  VAT_imaging_df,
  ASAT_imaging_df,
  TTMFI_imaging_df,
  TTFFMVi_imaging_df,
  Liver_PDFF_imaging_df,
  Pancreas_PDFF_imaging_df,
  Spine_BMD_imaging_df[,c(1,4)],
  Total_BMD_imaging_df[,c(1,4)],
  Android_fat_imaging_df, #DXA body composition variable - not necessary for the analyses
  Gynoid_fat_imaging_df, #DXA body composition variable - not necessary for the analyses
  Legs_fat_imaging_df, #DXA body composition variable - not necessary for the analyses
  Total_fat_imaging_df, #DXA body composition variable - not necessary for the analyses
  Trunk_fat_imaging_df #DXA body composition variable - not necessary for the analyses
)

#Or use simplified code below, both produce the same sample sized data frame (50,208)
data_frames <- list(
  Spine_BMFF_final_df[,c(1,4,9)],
  Age_sex_imaging_df,
  MRI_model_3_covariate_df,
  Spine_BMD_imaging_df[,c(1,4)],
  Total_BMD_imaging_df[,c(1,4)], #Add Total BMD and impute alongside other covariates for sensitivity analysis (06/10/2025)
  DXA_model_3_covariate_df
)


# Use Reduce to merge them one by one
Spine_BMFF_regressions_df <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = 'eid', all = TRUE), data_frames)

#Remove NAs from BMFF column only - and from sex and age columns (no NAs)
Spine_BMFF_regressions_df <- Spine_BMFF_regressions_df[!is.na(Spine_BMFF_regressions_df$rt_BMFF), ]
Spine_BMFF_regressions_df <- Spine_BMFF_regressions_df[!is.na(Spine_BMFF_regressions_df$Sex), ]
Spine_BMFF_regressions_df <- Spine_BMFF_regressions_df[!is.na(Spine_BMFF_regressions_df$Age_first_second_imaging), ]

#Check the quantity of missing outcome variables

total_sample_size <- 50208

# List of column names to check for missing values
columns_to_check <- c(
  "BMI_first_second_imaging",
  "WHR_first_second_imaging",
  "VATi_first_second_imaging",
  "ASATi_first_second_imaging",
  "TTMFI_first_second_imaging",
  "TTFFMVi_first_second_imaging",
  "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging",
  "Liver_PDFF_first_second_imaging",
  "Spine_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  "Total_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  "Android_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Gynoid_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Legs_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Trunk_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Total_fat_first_second_imaging"
)

# For loop to count missing values and calculate percentage
for (column in columns_to_check) {
  missing_count <- sum(is.na(Spine_BMFF_regressions_df[[column]]))
  missing_percentage <- (missing_count / total_sample_size) * 100
  cat(column, ": ", missing_count, " missing values (", round(missing_percentage, 2), "%)\n")
}

#BMI_first_second_imaging :  1216  missing values ( 2.42 %)
#WHR_first_second_imaging :  1048  missing values ( 2.09 %)
#VATi_first_second_imaging :  119  missing values ( 0.24 %)
#ASATi_first_second_imaging :  217  missing values ( 0.43 %)
#TTMFI_first_second_imaging :  932  missing values ( 1.86 %)
#TTFFMVi_first_second_imaging :  785  missing values ( 1.56 %)
#Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging :  21459  missing values ( 42.74 %)
#Liver_PDFF_first_second_imaging :  10641  missing values ( 21.19 %)
#Spine_BMD_first_second_imaging :  7465  missing values ( 14.87 %)
#Total_BMD_first_second_imaging :  7460  missing values ( 14.86 %)
#Android_fat_first_second_imaging :  7457  missing values ( 14.85 %)
#Gynoid_fat_first_second_imaging :  7457  missing values ( 14.85 %)
#Legs_fat_first_second_imaging :  7457  missing values ( 14.85 %)
#Trunk_fat_first_second_imaging :  7457  missing values ( 14.85 %)
#Total_fat_first_second_imaging :  7457  missing values ( 14.85 %)

# Display the pattern of missing data
vis_miss(Spine_BMFF_regressions_df[,6:11],cluster = TRUE)

#Impute missing values <10% and Spine BMD and Total BMD
variables_to_impute <- c("BMI_first_second_imaging", "WHR_first_second_imaging", "VATi_first_second_imaging", "ASATi_first_second_imaging", "TTMFI_first_second_imaging", "TTFFMVi_first_second_imaging", "Spine_BMD_first_second_imaging", "Total_BMD_first_second_imaging")

# Perform multiple imputation
imputed_data <- mice(Spine_BMFF_regressions_df[variables_to_impute], m = 5, method = 'pmm', maxit = 50, seed = 123)

# To check the imputed data, you can use:
summary(imputed_data)

# If you want to complete the data with imputed values, use:
completed_data <- complete(imputed_data, action = "long")
completed_data = completed_data[-c(9,10)]

# Replace original NA values with imputed values
Imp_Spine_BMFF_regressions_df = Spine_BMFF_regressions_df
Imp_Spine_BMFF_regressions_df[variables_to_impute] <- completed_data

#Density plots
plot_list <- list()

# Loop over each variable and create a density plot
for (var in variables_to_impute) {
  plot <- ggplot() +
    geom_density(aes_string(x = paste0("Imp_Spine_BMFF_regressions_df$", var)), color = "blue") +
    geom_density(aes_string(x = paste0("Spine_BMFF_regressions_df$", var)), color = "red") +
    xlab(var) +
    ylab("Density") +
    theme_bw() +
    ggtitle(paste("Density Plot for", var))
  
  # Add the plot to the list
  plot_list[[var]] <- plot
}

# Print each plot
for (var in variables_to_impute) {
  print(plot_list[[var]])
}

export(Imp_Spine_BMFF_regressions_df , 'Imp_Spine_BMFF_regressions_df.csv')
```

2.2 Femoral head BMFF
```{r}
Femoral_head_BMFF_final_df$rt_BMFF = RankNorm(Femoral_head_BMFF_final_df$Mean.fat)

# Merge all of the outcome variables with Femoral_head_BMFF into one data frame
data_frames <- list(
  Femoral_head_BMFF_final_df[,c(1,4,9)],
  Age_sex_imaging_df,
  BMI_imaging_df,
  WHR_imaging_df,
  VAT_imaging_df,
  ASAT_imaging_df,
  TTMFI_imaging_df,
  TTFFMVi_imaging_df,
  Liver_PDFF_imaging_df,
  Pancreas_PDFF_imaging_df,
  Femur_neck_BMD_imaging_df[,c(1,4)],
  Android_fat_imaging_df,
  Gynoid_fat_imaging_df,
  Legs_fat_imaging_df,
  Total_fat_imaging_df,
  Trunk_fat_imaging_df
)

Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df <- Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df[!is.na(Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df$rt_BMFF), ]

Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df <- Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df[!is.na(Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df$Age_first_second_imaging), ]

# Check the quantity of missing outcome variables

total_sample_size <- 47029

# List of column names to check for missing values
columns_to_check <- c(
  "BMI_first_second_imaging",
  "WHR_first_second_imaging",
  "VATi_first_second_imaging",
  "ASATi_first_second_imaging",
  "TTMFI_first_second_imaging",
  "TTFFMVi_first_second_imaging",
  "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging",
  "Liver_PDFF_first_second_imaging",
  "Femur_neck_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  "Android_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Gynoid_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Legs_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Trunk_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Total_fat_first_second_imaging"
)

# For loop to count missing values and calculate percentage
for (column in columns_to_check) {
  missing_count <- sum(is.na(Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df[[column]]))
  missing_percentage <- (missing_count / total_sample_size) * 100
  cat(column, ": ", missing_count, " missing values (", round(missing_percentage, 2), "%)\n")
}


#BMI_first_second_imaging :  1126  missing values ( 2.24 %)
#WHR_first_second_imaging :  979  missing values ( 1.95 %)
#VATi_first_second_imaging :  76  missing values ( 0.15 %)
#ASATi_first_second_imaging :  175  missing values ( 0.35 %)
#TTMFI_first_second_imaging :  751  missing values ( 1.5 %)
#TTFFMVi_first_second_imaging :  613  missing values ( 1.22 %)
#Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging :  20038  missing values ( 39.91 %)
#Liver_PDFF_first_second_imaging :  9834  missing values ( 19.59 %)
#Femur_neck_BMD_first_second_imaging :  6907  missing values ( 13.76 %)
#Android_fat_first_second_imaging :  6939  missing values ( 13.82 %)
#Gynoid_fat_first_second_imaging :  6939  missing values ( 13.82 %)
#Legs_fat_first_second_imaging :  6939  missing values ( 13.82 %)
#Trunk_fat_first_second_imaging :  6939  missing values ( 13.82 %)
#Total_fat_first_second_imaging :  6939  missing values ( 13.82 %)

# Display the pattern of missing data
vis_miss(Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df[,6:11], cluster = TRUE)

# Impute missing values <10% and Spine BMD
variables_to_impute <- c("BMI_first_second_imaging", "WHR_first_second_imaging", "VATi_first_second_imaging", "ASATi_first_second_imaging", "TTMFI_first_second_imaging", "TTFFMVi_first_second_imaging", "Femur_neck_BMD_first_second_imaging")

# Perform multiple imputation
imputed_data <- mice(Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df[variables_to_impute], m = 5, method = 'pmm', maxit = 50, seed = 123)

# To check the imputed data, you can use:
summary(imputed_data)

# If you want to complete the data with imputed values, use:
completed_data <- complete(imputed_data, action = "long")
completed_data = completed_data[-c(1,2)]

# Replace original NA values with imputed values
Imp_Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df = Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df
Imp_Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df[variables_to_impute] <- completed_data

#Density plots
plot_list <- list()

# Loop over each variable and create a density plot
for (var in variables_to_impute) {
  plot <- ggplot() +
    geom_density(aes_string(x = paste0("Imp_Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df$", var)), color = "blue") +
    geom_density(aes_string(x = paste0("Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df$", var)), color = "red") +
    xlab(var) +
    ylab("Density") +
    theme_bw() +
    ggtitle(paste("Density Plot for", var))
  
  # Add the plot to the list
  plot_list[[var]] <- plot
}

# Print each plot
for (var in variables_to_impute) {
  print(plot_list[[var]])
}
```

2.3 Total hip
```{r}
Total_hip_BMFF_final_df$rt_BMFF = RankNorm(Total_hip_BMFF_final_df$Mean.fat)

# Merge all of the outcome variables with Total_hip_BMFF into one data frame
data_frames <- list(
  Total_hip_BMFF_final_df[,c(1,4,9)],
  Age_sex_imaging_df,
  BMI_imaging_df,
  WHR_imaging_df,
  VAT_imaging_df,
  ASAT_imaging_df,

  Liver_PDFF_imaging_df,
  Pancreas_PDFF_imaging_df,
  Femur_troch_BMD_imaging_df[,c(1,4)],

  Gynoid_fat_imaging_df,
  Legs_fat_imaging_df,
  Total_fat_imaging_df,
  Trunk_fat_imaging_df
)

  "VATi_first_second_imaging",
  "ASATi_first_second_imaging",
  "TTMFI_first_second_imaging",
  "TTFFMVi_first_second_imaging",
  "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging",
  "Liver_PDFF_first_second_imaging",
  "Femur_troch_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  "Android_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Gynoid_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Legs_fat_first_second_imaging",

  cat(column, ": ", missing_count, " missing values (", round(missing_percentage, 2), "%)\n")
}
```

2.4 Diaphysis
```{r}
Diaphysis_BMFF_final_df$rt_BMFF = RankNorm(Diaphysis_BMFF_final_df$Mean.fat)

# Merge all of the outcome variables with Diaphysis_BMFF into one data frame
data_frames <- list(
  Diaphysis_BMFF_final_df[,c(1,4,9)],
  Age_sex_imaging_df,
  BMI_imaging_df,
  WHR_imaging_df,
  VAT_imaging_df,
  ASAT_imaging_df,
  TTMFI_imaging_df,
  TTFFMVi_imaging_df,
  Liver_PDFF_imaging_df,
  Pancreas_PDFF_imaging_df,
  Femur_shaft_BMD_imaging_df[,c(1,4)],
  Android_fat_imaging_df,
  Gynoid_fat_imaging_df,
  Legs_fat_imaging_df,
  Total_fat_imaging_df,
  Trunk_fat_imaging_df
)

# Use Reduce to merge them one by one
Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = 'eid', all = TRUE), data_frames)

# Remove NAs from BMFF column only - and from sex and age columns (no NAs)
Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df <- Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df[!is.na(Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df$rt_BMFF), ]
Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df <- Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df[!is.na(Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df$Sex), ]
Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df <- Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df[!is.na(Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df$Age_first_second_imaging), ]

total_sample_size <- 45717

# List of column names to check for missing values
columns_to_check <- c(
  "BMI_first_second_imaging",
  "WHR_first_second_imaging",
  "VATi_first_second_imaging",
  "ASATi_first_second_imaging",
  "TTMFI_first_second_imaging",
  "TTFFMVi_first_second_imaging",
  "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging",
  "Liver_PDFF_first_second_imaging",
  "Femur_shaft_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  "Android_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Gynoid_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Legs_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Trunk_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Total_fat_first_second_imaging"
)

# For loop to count missing values and calculate percentage
for (column in columns_to_check) {
  missing_count <- sum(is.na(Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df[[column]]))
  missing_percentage <- (missing_count / total_sample_size) * 100
  cat(column, ": ", missing_count, " missing values (", round(missing_percentage, 2), "%)\n")
}

#BMI_first_second_imaging :  1130  missing values ( 2.25 %)
#WHR_first_second_imaging :  976  missing values ( 1.94 %)
#VATi_first_second_imaging :  87  missing values ( 0.17 %)
#ASATi_first_second_imaging :  183  missing values ( 0.36 %)
#TTMFI_first_second_imaging :  706  missing values ( 1.41 %)
#TTFFMVi_first_second_imaging :  583  missing values ( 1.16 %)
#Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging :  19441  missing values ( 38.72 %)
#Liver_PDFF_first_second_imaging :  9392  missing values ( 18.71 %)
#Femur_shaft_BMD_first_second_imaging :  6664  missing values ( 13.27 %)
#Android_fat_first_second_imaging :  6641  missing values ( 13.23 %)
#Gynoid_fat_first_second_imaging :  6641  missing values ( 13.23 %)
#Legs_fat_first_second_imaging :  6641  missing values ( 13.23 %)
#Trunk_fat_first_second_imaging :  6641  missing values ( 13.23 %)
#Total_fat_first_second_imaging :  6641  missing values ( 13.23 %)


# Display the pattern of missing data
vis_miss(Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df[,6:11], cluster = TRUE)

# Impute missing values <10% and Spine BMD
variables_to_impute <- c("BMI_first_second_imaging", "WHR_first_second_imaging", "VATi_first_second_imaging", "ASATi_first_second_imaging", "TTMFI_first_second_imaging", "TTFFMVi_first_second_imaging", "Femur_shaft_BMD_first_second_imaging")

# Perform multiple imputation
imputed_data <- mice(Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df[variables_to_impute], m = 5, method = 'pmm', maxit = 50, seed = 123)

# To check the imputed data, you can use:
summary(imputed_data)

# If you want to complete the data with imputed values, use:
completed_data <- complete(imputed_data, action = "long")
completed_data = completed_data[-c(1,2)]

# Replace original NA values with imputed values
Imp_Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df = Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df
Imp_Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df[variables_to_impute] <- completed_data

#Density plots
plot_list <- list()

# Loop over each variable and create a density plot
for (var in variables_to_impute) {
  plot <- ggplot() +
    geom_density(aes_string(x = paste0("Imp_Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df$", var)), color = "blue") +
    geom_density(aes_string(x = paste0("Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df$", var)), color = "red") +
    xlab(var) +
    ylab("Density") +
    theme_bw() +
    ggtitle(paste("Density Plot for", var))
  
  # Add the plot to the list
  plot_list[[var]] <- plot
}

# Print each plot
for (var in variables_to_impute) {
  print(plot_list[[var]])
}

export(Imp_Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df , 'Imp_Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df.csv')
```

2.5 Multi-site data frame
--> 2.5.1 Create multi-site BMFF data frame and run linear regressions between each multi-site measure and regional BMFF (including correlogram)
--> 2.5.2 Test differences in each multi-site measurement between maximally divergent spine and femoral region BMFF groups using ANOVAs and create plot
--> 2.5.3 Merge multi-site data frame with covariates and impute 
```{r}
#2.5.3 Multi-site measurements, linear regressions, correlogram

#PC1
BMFF_PCA_df$rt_PC1 = RankNorm(BMFF_PCA_df$PC1)

#PC2
BMFF_PCA_df$rt_PC2 = RankNorm(BMFF_PCA_df$PC2)

#Spine:Femoral ratio 
BMFF_PCA_df$Mean_femoral <- rowMeans(BMFF_PCA_df[, c("Femoral_head_BMFF", "Total_hip_BMFF", "Diaphysis_BMFF")], na.rm = TRUE)
BMFF_PCA_df$rt_mean_femoral_BMFF = RankNorm(BMFF_PCA_df$Mean_femoral)
BMFF_PCA_df$Spine_Femoral_ratio = BMFF_PCA_df$Spine_BMFF/BMFF_PCA_df$Mean_femoral
BMFF_PCA_df$rt_Spine_Femoral_ratio = RankNorm(BMFF_PCA_df$Spine_Femoral_ratio)

#Multi-site 1: Residual * ((Femoral BMFF geometric mean Z-score)^2 /Spine BMFF)
mean_femoral_spine_lm = BMFF_PCA_df %>% lm(rt_mean_femoral_BMFF~rt_Spine_BMFF, data=.)
Residuals_df = as.data.frame(mean_femoral_spine_lm$residuals)
colnames(Residuals_df) = 'Femoral_Spine_BMFF_residuals'
BMFF_PCA_df = cbind(BMFF_PCA_df, Residuals_df)
BMFF_PCA_df = BMFF_PCA_df %>% mutate(z_Mean_Femoral_BMFF = (BMFF_PCA_df$Mean_femoral- mean(BMFF_PCA_df$Mean_femoral))/sd(BMFF_PCA_df$Mean_femoral))
sd(BMFF_PCA_df$z_Mean_Femoral_BMFF)
mean(BMFF_PCA_df$z_Mean_Femoral_BMFF)

BMFF_PCA_df = BMFF_PCA_df %>% mutate(Multi_site_1 = Femoral_Spine_BMFF_residuals*(z_Mean_Femoral_BMFF^2/Spine_BMFF))
BMFF_PCA_df$rt_Multi_site_1 = RankNorm(BMFF_PCA_df$Multi_site_1)

export(BMFF_PCA_df, 'BMFF_PCA_df.csv')

#Spine BMFF
BMFF_PCA_df$rt_Spine_BMFF = RankNorm(BMFF_PCA_df$Spine_BMFF)

#Femoral head BMFF
BMFF_PCA_df$rt_Femoral_head_BMFF = RankNorm(BMFF_PCA_df$Femoral_head_BMFF)

#Total hip BMFF
BMFF_PCA_df$rt_Total_hip_BMFF = RankNorm(BMFF_PCA_df$Total_hip_BMFF)

#Diaphysis BMFF
BMFF_PCA_df$rt_Diaphysis_BMFF = RankNorm(BMFF_PCA_df$Diaphysis_BMFF)

#Merge with covariates (model 1, 2 and 3) and T2D outcomes from code chunks below (for manuscript)
BMFF_PCA_df_covariates = merge(BMFF_PCA_df[,-c(19:23)], Cohort_characteristics_df[,c(1,20,23,29:37,39,41,43)], by='eid')
colnames(BMFF_PCA_df_covariates)[c(10:13,34:37)] = c('PC1x', 'PC2x', 'PC3x', 'PC4x', 'PC1', 'PC2', 'PC3', 'PC4')

#Subset by sex and re-run for sex-specific regressions
BMFF_PCA_df_covariates = subset(BMFF_PCA_df_covariates, Sex=='M')
BMFF_PCA_df_covariates = subset(BMFF_PCA_df_covariates, Sex=='F')

#Run lms and create a summary statistics table
library(dplyr)
library(broom)

# Define outcomes and exposures
outcomes <- c("rt_PC1", "rt_PC2", "rt_Spine_Femoral_ratio", "rt_Multi_site_1")
exposures <- c("rt_Spine_BMFF", "rt_Femoral_head_BMFF", "rt_Total_hip_BMFF", "rt_Diaphysis_BMFF")

# Initialize empty list to collect results per outcome
results_list <- list()

for (out in outcomes) {
  # Build a data frame to hold results for this outcome across exposures
  res <- tibble(
    Exposures = exposures,
    Beta = NA_real_,
    CI_Lower = NA_real_,
    CI_Upper = NA_real_,
    R2 = NA_real_,
    p_value = NA_real_
  )
  
  for (i in seq_along(exposures)) {
    exp <- exposures[i]
    # Fit simple linear model: outcome ~ exposure
    formula <- as.formula(paste(out, "~", exp))
    fit <- lm(formula, data = BMFF_PCA_df_covariates)
    coef_summary <- summary(fit)$coefficients
    # The row for the predictor (second row if intercept is present)
    beta <- coef_summary[2, "Estimate"]
    pval <- coef_summary[2, "Pr(>|t|)"]
    # 95% CI for beta
    ci <- confint(fit, parm = exp, level = 0.95)
    # R-squared
    rsq <- summary(fit)$r.squared
    
    # Store results
    res$Beta[i] <- beta
    res$CI_Lower[i] <- ci[1, 1]
    res$CI_Upper[i] <- ci[1, 2]
    res$R2[i] <- rsq
    res$p_value[i] <- pval
  }
  
  # Save per-outcome results with the outcome name as the list element
  results_list[[out]] <- res
}

# Combine into a single data frame (long-ish tidy format)
results_df <- bind_rows(lapply(names(results_list), function(nm) {
  tmp <- results_list[[nm]]
  tmp <- tmp %>% mutate(Outcome = nm)
  tmp
}), .id = NULL)

# Create a single string for Beta with CI
results_df <- results_df %>%
  mutate(
    Beta_CI = paste0(round(Beta, 3),
                     " (",
                     round(CI_Lower, 3),
                     ", ",
                     round(CI_Upper, 3),
                     ")"),
    R2 = round(R2, 3)
  ) %>%
  select(Outcome, Exposures, Beta_CI, R2, p_value)

# Ensure exposures are ordered as desired
exposure_order <- c("rt_Spine_BMFF", "rt_Femoral_head_BMFF", "rt_Total_hip_BMFF", "rt_Diaphysis_BMFF")
results_df <- results_df %>%
  mutate(Exposures = factor(Exposures, levels = exposure_order)) %>%
  arrange(Outcome, Exposures)

# Wide format: Beta_CI, R2, and p_value per exposure
result_wide <- results_df %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = Outcome,
    names_from = Exposures,
    values_from = c(Beta_CI, R2, p_value)
  )

result_wide = result_wide[,c(1,2,6,10,3,7,11,4,8,12,5,9,13)]

#Correlogram
BMFF_PCA_df$rt_PC1 = RankNorm(BMFF_PCA_df$PC1)
BMFF_multi_site_corr_df = BMFF_PCA_df[,c(14:18,27,31,32)]
BMFF_multi_site_corr_df = cor(BMFF_multi_site_corr_df)

# Calculate the R-squared values
R_squared <- BMFF_multi_site_corr_df^2

# Get p-values for the correlation tests
BMFF_multi_site_p_corr <- corr.test(BMFF_multi_site_corr_df)$p
BMFF_multi_site_p_corr

# Create the corrplot using R-squared values for coloring
corrplot(BMFF_multi_site_corr_df, 
         method = 'color', 
         type = 'upper',
         sig.level = c(0.00001, 0.0001, 0.001), 
         pch.cex = 0.9,
         insig = 'label_sig', 
         pch.col = 'grey20', 
         tl.srt = 45, 
         addCoef.col = 'grey50',
         diag = FALSE,
         )

#2.5.2 Test differenes between spine - femoral regions BMFF groups using ANOVAs and plot

#Create spine BMFF z-score 
BMFF_PCA_df = BMFF_PCA_df %>% mutate(z_Spine_BMFF = (BMFF_PCA_df$Spine_BMFF- mean(BMFF_PCA_df$Spine_BMFF))/sd(BMFF_PCA_df$Spine_BMFF))

#lms
mean_femoral_spine_lm = BMFF_PCA_df %>% lm(rt_mean_femoral_BMFF~rt_Spine_BMFF, data=.)
Residuals_df = as.data.frame(mean_femoral_spine_lm$residuals)
mean_femoral_spine_lm$residuals

BMFF_flag_plot_df = BMFF_PCA_df[,c(1,2,14,20,23:26)]
BMFF_flag_plot_df = cbind(BMFF_flag_plot_df, Residuals_df)
BMFF_flag_plot_df$Residuals = as.numeric(BMFF_flag_plot_df$`mean_femoral_spine_lm$residuals`)
colnames(BMFF_flag_plot_df)[9] = "Residuals"
BMFF_flag_plot_df = BMFF_flag_plot_df %>% mutate(z_Spine_BMFF = (BMFF_PCA_df$Spine_BMFF- mean(BMFF_PCA_df$Spine_BMFF))/sd(BMFF_PCA_df$Spine_BMFF))

BMFF_flag_plot_df = BMFF_PCA_df_covariates
colnames(BMFF_flag_plot_df)[29] = 'Residuals'

#stratify groups according to Spine BMFF z-score and residuals
BMFF_flag_plot_df <- BMFF_flag_plot_df %>%
  mutate(Flag_plot_strata = case_when(
    z_Spine_BMFF < -1 & Residuals < -1 ~ "LowF_LowS",
    z_Spine_BMFF > 1 & Residuals < -1 ~ "LowF_HighS",
    z_Spine_BMFF < -1 & Residuals > 1 ~ "HighF_LowS",
    z_Spine_BMFF > 1 & Residuals > 1 ~ "HighF_HighS"
  ))

LowF_LowS = subset(BMFF_flag_plot_df, BMFF_flag_plot_df$Flag_plot_strata =="LowF_LowS")
LowF_HighS = subset(BMFF_flag_plot_df, BMFF_flag_plot_df$Flag_plot_strata =="LowF_HighS")
HighF_LowS = subset(BMFF_flag_plot_df, BMFF_flag_plot_df$Flag_plot_strata =="HighF_LowS")
HighF_HighS = subset(BMFF_flag_plot_df, BMFF_flag_plot_df$Flag_plot_strata =="HighF_HighS")

BMFF_flag_plot_df %>% ggplot(aes(y=rt_mean_femoral_BMFF, x=rt_Spine_BMFF)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = (Residuals)))+
  geom_point(data=LowF_LowS, aes(x=rt_Spine_BMFF, y=rt_mean_femoral_BMFF), colour="darkred")+
  geom_point(data=LowF_HighS, aes(x=rt_Spine_BMFF, y=rt_mean_femoral_BMFF), colour="darkblue")+
  geom_point(data=HighF_LowS, aes(x=rt_Spine_BMFF, y=rt_mean_femoral_BMFF), colour="darkorange")+
  geom_point(data=HighF_HighS, aes(x=rt_Spine_BMFF, y=rt_mean_femoral_BMFF), colour="black")+
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "gray")+
  labs(y="Normalized mean femoral BMFF", x="Normalized spine BMFF")+
  theme_bw()+
  scale_color_continuous("Residuals", low = "#40E0D0", high = "#00008B")+
  theme(title = element_text(face = "bold", size=12))+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))+
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold")) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold")) +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold")) +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))

#Test differences between groups and get the ANOVA effect estimates using sjstats
library(sjstats)
flag_plot_aov = BMFF_flag_plot_df %>% aov(rt_PC2~Flag_plot_strata, data=.)
summary(flag_plot_aov)
omega_F = anova_stats(flag_plot_aov) #p<2e16
omega_F$omegasq #0.885
omega_F$statistic #10340

BMFF_flag_plot_df %>%
  filter(Flag_plot_strata=="LowF_LowS") %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(PC1, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_value = sd(PC1, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

BMFF_flag_plot_df %>%
  filter(Sex=="M"&Flag_plot_strata=="LowF_LowS") %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(PC1, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_value = sd(PC1, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

BMFF_flag_plot_df %>%
  filter(Sex=="F"&Flag_plot_strata=="LowF_LowS") %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(PC1, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_value = sd(PC1, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

BMFF_flag_plot_df = na.omit(BMFF_flag_plot_df)

##---------------------------------
## Run ANOVA + summary stats + median-IQR
##---------------------------------

library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(sjstats)

measure_vars <- c(
  "rt_Multi_site_1",
  "rt_PC1",
  "rt_PC2",
  "rt_Spine_Femoral_ratio"
)

# Sex groups: "both" = no filtering by Sex
sex_groups <- c("both", "M", "F")

##---------------------------------
## 1) ANOVA summary per measure & sex
##    model: measure ~ Flag_plot_strata
##---------------------------------

anova_stats_df <- map_dfr(measure_vars, function(var) {
  map_dfr(sex_groups, function(sex_g) {
    
    # Subset by sex group
    df_sub <- if (sex_g == "both") {
      BMFF_flag_plot_df
    } else {
      BMFF_flag_plot_df %>% filter(Sex == sex_g)
    }
    
    # Remove missing values in outcome or grouping
    df_aov <- df_sub %>%
      filter(
        !is.na(.data[[var]]),
        !is.na(Flag_plot_strata)
      )
    
    # Need at least 2 strata levels and more than 1 row
    if (nrow(df_aov) < 2 || dplyr::n_distinct(df_aov$Flag_plot_strata) < 2) {
      return(NULL)
    }
    
    # Fit ANOVA using reformulate (avoids tilde in code)
    fit <- tryCatch(
      aov(reformulate("Flag_plot_strata", response = var), data = df_aov),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
    
    # Extract ANOVA stats
    st <- tryCatch(
      sjstats::anova_stats(fit),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(st) || !"term" %in% names(st)) return(NULL)
    
    # For a one-way ANOVA, the group effect is the non-residual term
    st_factor <- st[!grepl("Residual", st$term, ignore.case = TRUE) & !is.na(st$term), , drop = FALSE]
    if (nrow(st_factor) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    row <- st_factor[1, , drop = FALSE]
    
    # Extract columns (handles slight name variations)
    omega_val <- if ("omegasq"  %in% names(row)) as.numeric(row$omegasq)  else NA_real_
    F_val     <- if ("statistic" %in% names(row)) as.numeric(row$statistic) else NA_real_
    df_resid  <- if ("df.residual" %in% names(row)) as.numeric(row$df.residual) else NA_real_
    p_val     <- if ("p.value"   %in% names(row)) as.numeric(row$p.value)   else NA_real_
    df_val    <- if ("df"        %in% names(row)) as.numeric(row$df)        else NA_real_
    
    tibble(
      measure     = var,
      sex_group   = sex_g,   # "both", "M", "F"
      term        = as.character(row$term),
      df          = df_val,
      df_residual = df_resid,
      F_stat      = F_val,
      p_value     = p_val,
      omega_sq    = omega_val
    )
  })
})

##---------------------------------
## 2) Median ± IQR per measure, sex, and Flag_plot_strata
##---------------------------------

# Levels and measurements
flag_levels <- c("LowF_LowS", "LowF_HighS", "HighF_LowS", "HighF_HighS")
measurements <- c("Multi_site_1", "PC1", "PC2", "Spine_Femoral_ratio")

results <- data.frame(
  Flag_plot_strata = character(),
  measurement = character(),
  Sex = character(),
  median_value = numeric(),
  IQR_value = numeric(),
  result_str = character(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

for (lev in flag_levels) {
  for (m in measurements) {
    # Overall (All sexes)
    med_all <- median(BMFF_flag_plot_df %>% filter(Flag_plot_strata == lev) %>% pull(!!sym(m)), na.rm = TRUE)
    iqv_all <- sd(BMFF_flag_plot_df %>% filter(Flag_plot_strata == lev) %>% pull(!!sym(m)), na.rm = TRUE)
    results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
      Flag_plot_strata = lev,
      measurement = m,
      Sex = "All",
      median_value = med_all,
      IQR_value = iqv_all,
      result_str = paste0(round(med_all, 3), " \u00B1 ", round(iqv_all, 3)),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
    
    # By Sex: Male
    med_m <- median(BMFF_flag_plot_df %>% filter(Flag_plot_strata == lev, Sex == "M") %>% pull(!!sym(m)), na.rm = TRUE)
    iqv_m <- sd(BMFF_flag_plot_df %>% filter(Flag_plot_strata == lev, Sex == "M") %>% pull(!!sym(m)), na.rm = TRUE)
    results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
      Flag_plot_strata = lev,
      measurement = m,
      Sex = "M",
      median_value = med_m,
      IQR_value = iqv_m,
      result_str = paste0(round(med_m, 3), " \u00B1 ", round(iqv_m, 3)),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
    
    # By Sex: Female
    med_f <- median(BMFF_flag_plot_df %>% filter(Flag_plot_strata == lev, Sex == "F") %>% pull(!!sym(m)), na.rm = TRUE)
    iqv_f <- sd(BMFF_flag_plot_df %>% filter(Flag_plot_strata == lev, Sex == "F") %>% pull(!!sym(m)), na.rm = TRUE)
    results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
      Flag_plot_strata = lev,
      measurement = m,
      Sex = "F",
      median_value = med_f,
      IQR_value = iqv_f,
      result_str = paste0(round(med_f, 3), " \u00B1 ", round(iqv_f, 3)),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }
}

results


#2.5.3 Create multi-site data frame and impute covariates
PC2_final_df = BMFF_PCA_df

# Merge all of the outcome variables with PC2 into one data frame
data_frames <- list(
  PC2_final_df,
  Age_sex_imaging_df,
  BMI_imaging_df,
  WHR_imaging_df,
  VAT_imaging_df,
  ASAT_imaging_df,
  TTMFI_imaging_df,
  TTFFMVi_imaging_df,
  Liver_PDFF_imaging_df,
  Pancreas_PDFF_imaging_df,
  Total_BMD_imaging_df[,c(1,4)],
  Android_fat_imaging_df,
  Gynoid_fat_imaging_df,
  Legs_fat_imaging_df,
  Total_fat_imaging_df,
  Trunk_fat_imaging_df
)

# Use Reduce to merge them one by one
PC2_regressions_df <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = 'eid', all = TRUE), data_frames)

# Remove NAs from BMFF column only - and from sex and age columns (no NAs)
PC2_regressions_df <- PC2_regressions_df[!is.na(PC2_regressions_df$rt_PC2), ]
PC2_regressions_df <- PC2_regressions_df[!is.na(PC2_regressions_df$Sex), ]
PC2_regressions_df <- PC2_regressions_df[!is.na(PC2_regressions_df$Age_first_second_imaging), ]

total_sample_size <- 41873

# List of column names to check for missing values
columns_to_check <- c(
  "BMI_first_second_imaging",
  "WHR_first_second_imaging",
  "VATi_first_second_imaging",
  "ASATi_first_second_imaging",
  "TTMFI_first_second_imaging",
  "TTFFMVi_first_second_imaging",
  "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging",
  "Liver_PDFF_first_second_imaging",
  "Total_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  "Android_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Gynoid_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Legs_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Trunk_fat_first_second_imaging",
  "Total_fat_first_second_imaging"
)

# For loop to count missing values and calculate percentage
for (column in columns_to_check) {
  missing_count <- sum(is.na(PC2_regressions_df[[column]]))
  missing_percentage <- (missing_count / total_sample_size) * 100
  cat(column, ": ", missing_count, " missing values (", round(missing_percentage, 2), "%)\n")
}

#BMI_first_second_imaging :  1018  missing values ( 2.03 %)
#WHR_first_second_imaging :  895  missing values ( 1.78 %)
#VATi_first_second_imaging :  70  missing values ( 0.14 %)
#ASATi_first_second_imaging :  154  missing values ( 0.31 %)
#TTMFI_first_second_imaging :  557  missing values ( 1.11 %)
#TTFFMVi_first_second_imaging :  449  missing values ( 0.89 %)
#Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging :  17840  missing values ( 35.53 %)
#Liver_PDFF_first_second_imaging :  8510  missing values ( 16.95 %)
#Total_BMD_first_second_imaging :  6013  missing values ( 11.98 %)
#Android_fat_first_second_imaging :  6011  missing values ( 11.97 %)
#Gynoid_fat_first_second_imaging :  6011  missing values ( 11.97 %)
#Legs_fat_first_second_imaging :  6011  missing values ( 11.97 %)
#Trunk_fat_first_second_imaging :  6011  missing values ( 11.97 %)
#Total_fat_first_second_imaging :  6011  missing values ( 11.97 %)

# Display the pattern of missing data
vis_miss(PC2_regressions_df[,6:11], cluster = TRUE)

# Impute missing values <10% and Total BMD
variables_to_impute <- c("BMI_first_second_imaging", "WHR_first_second_imaging", "VATi_first_second_imaging", "ASATi_first_second_imaging", "TTMFI_first_second_imaging", "TTFFMVi_first_second_imaging", "Total_BMD_first_second_imaging")

# Perform multiple imputation
imputed_data <- mice(PC2_regressions_df[variables_to_impute], m = 5, method = 'pmm', maxit = 50, seed = 123)

# To check the imputed data, you can use:
summary(imputed_data)

# If you want to complete the data with imputed values, use:
completed_data <- complete(imputed_data, action = "long")
completed_data = completed_data[-c(1,2)]

# Replace original NA values with imputed values
Imp_PC2_regressions_df = PC2_regressions_df
Imp_PC2_regressions_df[variables_to_impute] <- completed_data

#Density plots

# Initialize an empty list to store plots
plot_list <- list()

# Loop over each variable and create a density plot
for (var in variables_to_impute) {
  plot <- ggplot() +
    geom_density(aes_string(x = paste0("Imp_PC2_regressions_df$", var)), color = "blue") +
    geom_density(aes_string(x = paste0("PC2_regressions_df$", var)), color = "red") +
    xlab(var) +
    ylab("Density") +
    theme_bw() +
    ggtitle(paste("Density Plot for", var))
  
  # Add the plot to the list
  plot_list[[var]] <- plot
}

# Print each plot
for (var in variables_to_impute) {
  print(plot_list[[var]])
}

export(Imp_PC2_regressions_df, 'Imp_PC2_regressions_df.csv')
```

3. Run linear regressions between anthropometric and body composition outcomes for regional BMFF and each multi-site measurement
--> 3.1 Linear regressions using non-transformed outcome variables ('Normal lms')
--> 3.2 Linear regressions using rank-transformed normalised outcome variables ('per-SD lms')
--> 3.3 Create forest plot panel figure
```{r}
#For all measurments apart from pancreas and liver PDFF, use the data frame names below and change the outcome variable in the linear regression forloops to create separate summary statistics data frames (i.e., one summary statistics data frame per outcome variable). !! Remove BMI as a covariate when running linear regressions with BMI as the outcome !!

#All multi-site measurements come from the same data frame
Imp_PC1_regressions_df = Imp_PC2_regressions_df 
Imp_Multi_site_1_regressions_df  = Imp_PC2_regressions_df 
Imp_S_F_ratio_regressions_df = Imp_PC2_regressions_df 

#Name columns (optional) for each regional BMFF data frame
colnames(Imp_Spine_BMFF_regressions_df)[2] = 'Spine_BMFF'
colnames(Imp_Spine_BMFF_regressions_df)[3] = 'rt_Spine_BMFF'

colnames(Imp_Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df)[2] = 'Femoral_head_BMFF'
colnames(Imp_Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df)[3] = 'rt_Femoral_head_BMFF'

colnames(Imp_Total_hip_BMFF_regressions_df)[2] = 'Total_hip_BMFF'
colnames(Imp_Total_hip_BMFF_regressions_df)[3] = 'rt_Total_hip_BMFF'

colnames(Imp_Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df)[2] = 'Diaphysis_BMFF'
colnames(Imp_Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df)[3] = 'rt_Diaphysis_BMFF'

#For Pancreas and Liver PDFF outcomes, make sure to subset the data frames to the smaller sample size with complete PDFF data - do this separately for Pancreas and Liver PDFF (i.e., replace 'Pancreas' with 'Liver' for the chunk below to get the data frames with complete Liver PDFF data)
Imp_PC2_regressions_df = Imp_PC2_regressions_df[!is.na(Imp_PC2_regressions_df$Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging),] 
Imp_PC1_regressions_df = Imp_PC2_regressions_df[!is.na(Imp_PC2_regressions_df$Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging),]
Imp_Multi_site_1_regressions_df  = Imp_PC2_regressions_df[!is.na(Imp_PC2_regressions_df$Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging),]
Imp_S_F_ratio_regressions_df = Imp_PC2_regressions_df[!is.na(Imp_PC2_regressions_df$Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging),] 
Imp_Spine_BMFF_regressions_df = Imp_Spine_BMFF_regressions_df[!is.na(Imp_Spine_BMFF_regressions_df$Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging),]
Imp_Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df = Imp_Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df[!is.na(Imp_Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df$Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging),]
Imp_Total_hip_BMFF_regressions_df = Imp_Total_hip_BMFF_regressions_df[!is.na(Imp_Total_hip_BMFF_regressions_df$Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging),]
Imp_Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df = Imp_Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df[!is.na(Imp_Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df$Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging),]


#3.1 Normal lms 
# Define the data frames and corresponding variable names
data_list <- list(
  Imp_PC2_regressions_df = "PC2",
  Imp_PC1_regressions_df = "PC1",
  Imp_Multi_site_1_regressions_df = "Multi_site_1",
  Imp_S_F_ratio_regressions_df = "Spine_Femoral_ratio",
  Imp_Spine_BMFF_regressions_df = "Spine_BMFF",
  Imp_Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df = "Femoral_head_BMFF",
  Imp_Total_hip_BMFF_regressions_df = "Total_hip_BMFF",
  Imp_Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df = "Diaphysis_BMFF"
)

# Define the BMD covariates for Model 2
bmd_covariates <- list(
  Imp_PC2_regressions_df = "Total_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  Imp_PC1_regressions_df = "Total_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  Imp_Multi_site_1_regressions_df = "Total_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  Imp_S_F_ratio_regressions_df = "Total_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  Imp_Spine_BMFF_regressions_df = "Spine_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  Imp_Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df = "Femur_neck_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  Imp_Total_hip_BMFF_regressions_df = "Femur_troch_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  Imp_Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df = "Femur_shaft_BMD_first_second_imaging"
)

# Function to extract results
extract_results <- function(model, data_subset, outcome_var) {
  coef_summary <- summary(model)$coefficients
  conf_int <- confint(model)
  r_squared <- summary(model)$r.squared
  n_participants <- sum(!is.na(data_subset[[outcome_var]]))
  data.frame(
    Variable = rownames(coef_summary),
    Estimate = coef_summary[, "Estimate"],
    `95% CI Lower` = conf_int[, 1],
    `95% CI Upper` = conf_int[, 2],
    `R-squared` = r_squared,
    `p-value` = coef_summary[, "Pr(>|t|)"],
    `N Participants` = n_participants
  )
}

# Create a list to store all results
all_results <- list()

# Loop over each data frame and variable - change the outcome variable name for the respective outcomes (e.g., Pancreas PDFF shown below)
for (data_name in names(data_list)) {
  exposure_var <- data_list[[data_name]]
  rt_exposure_var <- paste0("rt_", exposure_var)
  
  # Get the corresponding BMD covariate
  bmd_covariate <- bmd_covariates[[data_name]]

  data <- get(data_name)

  # Model 1
  model1 <- lm(Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging ~ get(rt_exposure_var) * Sex + Age_first_second_imaging + BMI_first_second_imaging, data = data)
  all_results[[paste0(data_name, "_model1")]] <- extract_results(model1, data, "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging")

  model1_female <- lm(Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging ~ get(rt_exposure_var) + Age_first_second_imaging+ BMI_first_second_imaging, data = subset(data, Sex == 'F'))
  all_results[[paste0(data_name, "_model1_female")]] <- extract_results(model1_female, subset(data, Sex == 'F'), "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging")

  model1_male <- lm(Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging ~ get(rt_exposure_var) + Age_first_second_imaging+ BMI_first_second_imaging, data = subset(data, Sex == 'M'))
  all_results[[paste0(data_name, "_model1_male")]] <- extract_results(model1_male, subset(data, Sex == 'M'), "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging")

  # Model 2 with specific BMD covariate
  model2 <- lm(Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging ~ get(rt_exposure_var) * Sex + Age_first_second_imaging + BMI_first_second_imaging+ get(bmd_covariate), data = data)
  all_results[[paste0(data_name, "_model2")]] <- extract_results(model2, data, "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging")

  model2_female <- lm(Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging ~ get(rt_exposure_var) + Age_first_second_imaging + BMI_first_second_imaging+ get(bmd_covariate), data = subset(data, Sex == 'F'))
  all_results[[paste0(data_name, "_model2_female")]] <- extract_results(model2_female, subset(data, Sex == 'F'), "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging")

  model2_male <- lm(Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging ~ get(rt_exposure_var) + Age_first_second_imaging + BMI_first_second_imaging+ get(bmd_covariate), data = subset(data, Sex == 'M'))
  all_results[[paste0(data_name, "_model2_male")]] <- extract_results(model2_male, subset(data, Sex == 'M'), "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging")
}

# Combine all results into a single data frame
all_results_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(all_results), function(name) {
  result_df <- all_results[[name]]
  result_df$Model <- name
  result_df
}))

# Filter and format results
all_results_df <- all_results_df %>%
  filter(Variable == 'get(rt_exposure_var)')

colnames(all_results_df)[c(3:7)] <- c("LL", "UL", "R_squared", "p", "n") 

all_results_df <- all_results_df %>%
  mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 3) %>%
  mutate(
    Beta_CI = paste0(
      round(Estimate, 3), " (",
      round(LL, 3), ", ",
      round(UL, 3), ")"
    )
  )

export(all_results_df, "BMFF_multi_site_Pancreas_PDFF_lms_2.xlsx")


#3.2 per-SD lms

# Define the data frames and corresponding variable names
data_list <- list(
  Imp_PC2_regressions_df = "PC2",
  Imp_PC1_regressions_df = "PC1",
  Imp_Multi_site_1_regressions_df = "Multi_site_1",
  Imp_S_F_ratio_regressions_df = "Spine_Femoral_ratio",
  Imp_Spine_BMFF_regressions_df = "Spine_BMFF",
  Imp_Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df = "Femoral_head_BMFF",
  Imp_Total_hip_BMFF_regressions_df = "Total_hip_BMFF",
  Imp_Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df = "Diaphysis_BMFF"
)

# Define the BMD covariates for Model 2
bmd_covariates <- list(
  Imp_PC2_regressions_df = "Total_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  Imp_PC1_regressions_df = "Total_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  Imp_Multi_site_1_regressions_df = "Total_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  Imp_S_F_ratio_regressions_df = "Total_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  Imp_Spine_BMFF_regressions_df = "Spine_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  Imp_Femoral_head_BMFF_regressions_df = "Femur_neck_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  Imp_Total_hip_BMFF_regressions_df = "Femur_troch_BMD_first_second_imaging",
  Imp_Diaphysis_BMFF_regressions_df = "Femur_shaft_BMD_first_second_imaging"
)

# Function to extract results
extract_results <- function(model, data_subset, outcome_var) {
  coef_summary <- summary(model)$coefficients
  conf_int <- confint(model)
  r_squared <- summary(model)$r.squared
  n_participants <- sum(!is.na(data_subset[[outcome_var]]))
  data.frame(
    Variable = rownames(coef_summary),
    Estimate = coef_summary[, "Estimate"],
    `95% CI Lower` = conf_int[, 1],
    `95% CI Upper` = conf_int[, 2],
    `R-squared` = r_squared,
    `p-value` = coef_summary[, "Pr(>|t|)"],
    `N Participants` = n_participants
  )
}

# Create a list to store all results
all_results <- list()

# Loop over each data frame and variable  - change the outcome variable name for the respective outcomes (e.g., Pancreas PDFF shown below)
for (data_name in names(data_list)) {
  exposure_var <- data_list[[data_name]]
  rt_exposure_var <- paste0("rt_", exposure_var)
  
  # Get the corresponding BMD covariate
  bmd_covariate <- bmd_covariates[[data_name]]

  data <- get(data_name)

  # Model 1
  model1 <- lm(RankNorm(Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging) ~ get(rt_exposure_var) * Sex + Age_first_second_imaging+ BMI_first_second_imaging, data = data)
  all_results[[paste0(data_name, "_model1")]] <- extract_results(model1, data, "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging")

  model1_female <- lm(RankNorm(Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging) ~ get(rt_exposure_var) + Age_first_second_imaging+ BMI_first_second_imaging, data = subset(data, Sex == 'F'))
  all_results[[paste0(data_name, "_model1_female")]] <- extract_results(model1_female, subset(data, Sex == 'F'), "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging")

  model1_male <- lm(RankNorm(Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging) ~ get(rt_exposure_var) + Age_first_second_imaging+ BMI_first_second_imaging, data = subset(data, Sex == 'M'))
  all_results[[paste0(data_name, "_model1_male")]] <- extract_results(model1_male, subset(data, Sex == 'M'), "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging")

  # Model 2 with specific BMD covariate
  model2 <- lm(RankNorm(Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging) ~ get(rt_exposure_var) * Sex + Age_first_second_imaging + BMI_first_second_imaging+ get(bmd_covariate), data = data)
  all_results[[paste0(data_name, "_model2")]] <- extract_results(model2, data, "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging")

  model2_female <- lm(RankNorm(Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging) ~ get(rt_exposure_var) + Age_first_second_imaging + BMI_first_second_imaging+ get(bmd_covariate), data = subset(data, Sex == 'F'))
  all_results[[paste0(data_name, "_model2_female")]] <- extract_results(model2_female, subset(data, Sex == 'F'), "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging")

  model2_male <- lm(RankNorm(Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging) ~ get(rt_exposure_var) + Age_first_second_imaging + BMI_first_second_imaging+ get(bmd_covariate), data = subset(data, Sex == 'M'))
  all_results[[paste0(data_name, "_model2_male")]] <- extract_results(model2_male, subset(data, Sex == 'M'), "Pancreas_PDFF_first_second_imaging")
}

# Combine all results into a single data frame
all_results_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(all_results), function(name) {
  result_df <- all_results[[name]]
  result_df$Model <- name
  result_df
}))

all_results_df <- all_results_df %>%
  filter(Variable == "scale(get(rt_exposure_var))")

colnames(all_results_df)[c(3:7)] = c("LL", "UL", "R_squared", "p", "n") 

all_results_df <- all_results_df %>%
  mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 3)

all_results_df <- all_results_df %>%
  mutate(
    Beta_CI = paste0(
      round(Estimate, 3), " (",
      round(LL, 3), ", ",
      round(UL, 3), ")"
    )
  )

export(all_results_df, "BMFF_multi_site_Pancreas_PDFF_lms.xlsx")

#3.3 Forest plot panel figure

# Packages
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(forcats)
library(glue)

#Load Excel data frame
Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df = Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df[!is.na(Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df$...2),]
Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df = Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df[,-3]
colnames(Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df) = c('Exposure', 'Outcome', 'Model_1_beta', 'Model_2_beta')
Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df = Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df[-1,]
Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df[1:8,1,] = 'Spine BMFF'
Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df[9:16,1,] = 'Femoral head BMFF'
Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df[17:24,1,] = 'Total hip BMFF'
Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df[25:32,1,] = 'Diaphysis BMFF'
Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df[33:40,1,] = 'PC2'

export(Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df, 'Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df.xlsx')

df_raw = Forest_plot_BC_LMs_df

# Panel forest plot (Both sexes only)
# Works with long or wide input, and with beta/CI as numbers or strings like "0.12 (0.05, 0.19)"

# Panel forest plot (Both sexes only) with robust detection and safe renaming

# ========== Packages ==========
library(tidyverse)   # dplyr, ggplot2, tidyr, stringr, forcats, readr
library(readxl)      # only if reading Excel
library(glue)

# ========== 1) LOAD DATA ==========
# df_raw <- read_csv("both_sexes_results.csv")
# or:
# df_raw <- read_excel("both_sexes_results.xlsx", sheet = 1)
df_raw <- read_csv("both_sexes_results.csv")

# ========== 2) ROBUST DETECTION + UNIFY TO LONG ==========

# Optional manual overrides (uncomment and set if needed)
# col_outcome <- "Outcome"
# col_exposure <- "Expsure"  # e.g., typo in your file
# col_model <- NA_character_
# col_beta  <- NA_character_
# col_lci   <- NA_character_
# col_uci   <- NA_character_
# col_m1    <- NA_character_
# col_m2    <- NA_character_

clean <- function(x) stringr::str_replace_all(stringr::str_to_lower(x), "[^a-z0-9]", "")
nm <- names(df_raw)
nm_clean <- clean(nm)
lut <- tibble(orig = nm, clean = nm_clean)

pick_by_clean <- function(pattern, require_beta = FALSE) {
  idx <- stringr::str_detect(lut$clean, pattern)
  if (require_beta) {
    idx <- idx & stringr::str_detect(lut$clean, "beta|rank|estimate|effect|coef|coefficient")
  }
  lut$orig[which(idx)]
}

# 2a) Detect Outcome and Exposure (handle typos like "Expsure")
if (!exists("col_outcome") || is.na(get0("col_outcome", ifnotfound = NA))) {
  cand_outcome <- pick_by_clean("^outcome$|^trait$|^phenotype$")
  col_outcome <- cand_outcome[1]
}
if (!exists("col_exposure") || is.na(get0("col_exposure", ifnotfound = NA))) {
  cand_exposure <- pick_by_clean("^exposure$|^expsure$|bmff|pc2")
  col_exposure <- cand_exposure[1]
}
if (is.na(col_outcome) || is.na(col_exposure)) {
  stop(glue("Could not detect Outcome/Exposure. Available columns: {toString(names(df_raw))}"))
}

# 2b) Try LONG format (Model + beta-like)
if (!exists("col_model") || is.na(get0("col_model", ifnotfound = NA))) {
  cand_model <- pick_by_clean("^model$")
  col_model <- cand_model[1]
}
if (!exists("col_beta") || is.na(get0("col_beta", ifnotfound = NA))) {
  cand_beta <- pick_by_clean("^beta$|ranknormalized.*beta|rank.?normalized.*beta|estimate|effect|coef|coefficient")
  col_beta <- cand_beta[1]
}
if (!exists("col_lci") || is.na(get0("col_lci", ifnotfound = NA))) {
  cand_lci <- pick_by_clean("(^|[^a-z])lci($|[^a-z])|lowerci|cilow|cilower|ci_lower|ci.low")
  col_lci <- cand_lci[1]
}
if (!exists("col_uci") || is.na(get0("col_uci", ifnotfound = NA))) {
  cand_uci <- pick_by_clean("(^|[^a-z])uci($|[^a-z])|upperci|cihigh|ciupper|ci_high|ci.high")
  col_uci <- cand_uci[1]
}

# 2c) Try WIDE format (Model 1 / Model 2) — also match "odel1"/"odel2" (missing 'm')
if (!exists("col_m1") || is.na(get0("col_m1", ifnotfound = NA))) {
  cand_m1 <- pick_by_clean("(m|)odel1", require_beta = TRUE)
  if (length(cand_m1) == 0) cand_m1 <- pick_by_clean("^m1$|m1beta|beta.?m1|^model_?1$|^odel_?1$", require_beta = TRUE)
  col_m1 <- cand_m1[1]
}
if (!exists("col_m2") || is.na(get0("col_m2", ifnotfound = NA))) {
  cand_m2 <- pick_by_clean("(m|)odel2", require_beta = TRUE)
  if (length(cand_m2) == 0) cand_m2 <- pick_by_clean("^m2$|m2beta|beta.?m2|^model_?2$|^odel_?2$", require_beta = TRUE)
  col_m2 <- cand_m2[1]
}

# Report detected columns (useful for debugging)
message("Detected columns:",
        "\n  Outcome:  ", col_outcome,
        "\n  Exposure: ", col_exposure,
        "\n  Long candidates -> Model: ", ifelse(is.na(col_model), "None", col_model),
        ", Beta: ", ifelse(is.na(col_beta), "None", col_beta),
        ", LCI: ", ifelse(is.na(col_lci), "None", col_lci),
        ", UCI: ", ifelse(is.na(col_uci), "None", col_uci),
        "\n  Wide candidates -> Model 1: ", ifelse(is.na(col_m1), "None", col_m1),
        ", Model 2: ", ifelse(is.na(col_m2), "None", col_m2)
)

parse_beta_ci <- function(x) {
  x <- stringr::str_squish(as.character(x))
  tibble(
    beta = as.numeric(stringr::str_match(x, "^([-+]?\\d*\\.?\\d+(?:[eE][-+]?\\d+)?)")[,2]),
    LCI  = as.numeric(stringr::str_match(x, "\\(([-+]?\\d*\\.?\\d+(?:[eE][-+]?\\d+)?)[,–-]")[,2]),
    UCI  = as.numeric(stringr::str_match(x, "[,–-]\\s*([-+]?\\d*\\.?\\d+(?:[eE][-+]?\\d+)?)\\)")[,2])
  )
}

# Helper to assert columns exist before renaming (avoids cryptic errors)
assert_has <- function(df, cols, label) {
  missing <- setdiff(cols, names(df))
  if (length(missing) > 0) {
    stop(glue("Cannot find {label} column(s): {toString(missing)}.\nAvailable: {toString(names(df))}"))
  }
}

# Unify to long: Outcome, Exposure, Model, beta, LCI, UCI
if (!is.na(col_model) && !is.na(col_beta)) {
  # LONG format
  assert_has(df_raw, c(col_outcome, col_exposure, col_model, col_beta), "required (long)")
  df <- df_raw %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      Outcome  = !!sym(col_outcome),
      Exposure = !!sym(col_exposure),
      Model    = !!sym(col_model),
      BetaLike = !!sym(col_beta)
    )

  if (!is.na(col_lci) && !is.na(col_uci) && all(c(col_lci, col_uci) %in% names(df_raw))) {
    df <- df %>%
      dplyr::rename(
        LCI = !!sym(col_lci),
        UCI = !!sym(col_uci)
      ) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        beta = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(BetaLike)),
        LCI  = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(LCI)),
        UCI  = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(UCI))
      )
    need_parse <- any(is.na(df$beta) | is.na(df$LCI) | is.na(df$UCI))
    if (need_parse) {
      parsed <- parse_beta_ci(df$BetaLike)
      df$beta <- parsed$beta
      df$LCI  <- parsed$LCI
      df$UCI  <- parsed$UCI
    }
  } else {
    parsed <- parse_beta_ci(df$BetaLike)
    df <- dplyr::bind_cols(df, parsed)
  }

} else if (!is.na(col_m1) && !is.na(col_m2)) {
  # WIDE format
  assert_has(df_raw, c(col_outcome, col_exposure, col_m1, col_m2), "required (wide)")
  df <- df_raw %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      Outcome  = !!sym(col_outcome),
      Exposure = !!sym(col_exposure),
      `Model 1` = !!sym(col_m1),
      `Model 2` = !!sym(col_m2)
    ) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = c(`Model 1`, `Model 2`),
      names_to = "Model",
      values_to = "BetaLike"
    )

  parsed <- parse_beta_ci(df$BetaLike)
  df <- dplyr::bind_cols(df, parsed)

} else {
  stop(paste0(
    "Could not detect model columns.\n",
    "- LONG format requires a 'Model' column + beta-like column (e.g., 'Rank-normalized outcome beta').\n",
    "- WIDE format requires 'Model 1' and 'Model 2' columns (strings like '0.12 (0.05, 0.19)' are OK).\n",
    "Available columns: ", toString(names(df_raw))
  ))
}

stopifnot(all(c("Outcome","Exposure","Model","beta","LCI","UCI") %in% names(df)))

#Plot

library(ggstance)

# Ensure Outcome facet order (redundant if set earlier)
outcome_order  <- c("BMI (kg/m2)","WHR","VATi (L/m2)","ASATi (L/m2)","TTMFI (%)","TTFFMVi (L/m2)","Liver PDFF (%)","Pancreas PDFF (%)")
exposure_order = c('Spine:Femoral BMFF ratio' , 'Multi site 1', 'PC1', 'PC2', 'Diaphysis BMFF', 'Total hip BMFF', 'Femoral head BMFF', 'Spine BMFF')
df_plot <- df %>%
  mutate(
    Outcome = factor(Outcome, levels = outcome_order),
    # Keep exposure order already set earlier via fct_relevel
    Exposure = factor(Exposure, levels = exposure_order),
    Model = factor(Model, levels = c("Model 1", "Model 2"))  # for legend/color order
  )

# Position dodge vertically (separates Model 1/2 within each Exposure row)
pdv <- position_dodgev(height = 0.99)

p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = beta, y = Exposure, color = Model, group = Model)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "dashed") +
  ggstance::geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = LCI, xmax = UCI), height = 0, linewidth = 0.7,
                           position = pdv, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_point(shape = 16, size = 2.8, position = pdv) +
  facet_wrap(~ Outcome, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Model 1" = "darkblue", "Model 2" = "darkred"),
    breaks = c("Model 1", "Model 2"),
    name = "Adj. model"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Beta (95% CI)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    #panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    #panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

p

ggsave("BC_LMs_forest_plot_figure.png", p, width = 11, height = 9, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```

4. T2D outcome ascertainment

Define admission and imaging incident cases using the following outcome ascertainment from UKB data category 1712:
- PheCode map v1.2 (ICD-10 and 9 derived: data category 2002)
- Primary health care data (data-field 42040; record table 1060)
- T2D diagnosed by physician (data-fields 2443 and 2976) + non-cancer illness disease codes (data-fields 20002 and 20009) + 
- Death registry (data fields 40001 [underlying/primary cause of death: ICD10 coded]; 40002 [contributory/secondary causes of death: ICD10 coded; 40000 [date of death])
